title: Polypodium pothifolium
intro: Polypodium pellucidum (also known as Polypodium pellucidum) is actually known as Polypodium pellucidum is not Polypodium pothifolium though, which is not a widely recognized species name. However,  Polypodium pellucidum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The Polypodium pellucidum plant is a type of leafy green fern that is commonly used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve skin health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium pellucidum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, and it is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in various traditional remedies.
taxonomy: Polypodium pothifolium is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This family is a group of vascular plants commonly known as polypod ferns. Polypodium pothifolium is further classified as a member of the genus Polypodium, which contains around 80 species of ferns. The species is native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The classification of Polypodium pothifolium is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
